name: MenubarItem
description: Menu/Menubar item molecule. Supports 2 hierarchy levels, 4 variants (Default/Destructive/Radio/Checkbox), 3 states. Composes Lucide icon + label + optional keyboard shortcut.

variantConstraints.reason: Level=1 items don't get Radio or Checkbox variants — those are reserved for nested sub-menu items at Level=2.

$matrixNote: Raw matrix 2 × 4 × 3 = 24. variantConstraints removes 6 (Level=1 × Radio/Checkbox × 3 states) → 18 cells.

exposedProps.name: shortcutText
exposedProps.$comment: Keyboard shortcut. Uses Unicode glyphs in default — NOT Kbd component (different from Button KbdGroup).

exposedProps.name: showIcon
exposedProps.$comment: Icon slot exclusive to Default + Destructive variants. Radio/Checkbox use indent for active-indicator instead.

exposedProps.name: active
exposedProps.$comment: Active state for Radio/Checkbox. When true, shows Lucide Check at left in the indent space.

slots.name: icon
slots.$role: Left icon (16×16). Default + Destructive variants only.

slots.name: activeIndicator
slots.$role: Active state for Radio/Checkbox variants. 16×16 absolute-positioned in the left-indent space.

a11y.preferredElement: <div role='menuitem'> for Default/Destructive; <div role='menuitemradio' aria-checked={active}> for Radio; <div role='menuitemcheckbox' aria-checked={active}> for Checkbox
a11y.$keyboardShortcutA11y: Shortcut text is decorative — actual key binding handled by app keyboard handler. Add aria-keyshortcuts attribute if appropriate.

$new_audit_findings.F30: NEW (LOW). MenubarItem uses Unicode glyphs for shortcut text (⌘P), NOT the Kbd component (which Button uses for ⌘F display). Inconsistency within Apollo v2's keyboard-shortcut rendering convention.
$new_audit_findings.F31: NEW (DOC). Radio/Checkbox variants change the slot layout structure (replace icon slot with indent+active-indicator). First PoC where variant axis changes SLOT TOPOLOGY, not just styling. Validates SP-1 whenVariant filter pattern.

$step2_known_gaps.field: Hover state full styling — bg + text-color confirmation
Checkbox variant exact rendering (likely same as Radio with same Check icon — or different glyph?)
